title: andira retusa
intro: Andira retusa, also known as the "Guacatonga" tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Andira retusa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiproliferative effects against certain types of cancer cells. The herbal preparations of Andira retusa typically involve drying and grinding the leaves into a powder, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or capsules, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal dosages.
